page: Amazon Web Services m8g.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores utilizing the 64-bit ARM architecture with the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-threaded performance, particularly in text processing and data compression tasks. The single-threaded gzip compression reached 30.34 GB/sec, and the single-threaded bzip3 decompression scored 14.31 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. Passmark memory benchmarks rank this instance in the top 10% tier with a score of 3082.18, supported by a cached read-only memory bandwidth of 925.03 GB/sec. However, cryptographic performance is a relative bottleneck, with the Passmark CPU encryption test scoring in the bottom 25% tier at 1504.19. Large-model LLM inference is also limited, as prompt processing for a 7B model scored in the bottom 10% tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based Graviton architecture offers a balanced resource density of compute and memory without the overhead of hyperthreading, as each of the 8 vCPUs corresponds to a dedicated physical core. This makes the m8g.2xlarge highly suitable for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, redis caching, software compilation, and database operations. It is less suited for heavy cryptographic processing or large-scale machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8g.2xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance powered by an 8-core AWS Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, with a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps and no local storage or GPU. Performance benchmarks highlight strong single-threaded capabilities, with top-tier memory performance and strong results in text processing and compression. However, cryptographic operations and medium-model LLM prompt processing represent notable performance tradeoffs, scoring in the lower tiers. This instance delivers a balanced resource profile, making it a cost-efficient choice for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and caching, while avoiding highly intensive cryptographic or machine learning tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.2xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 4 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for balanced workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.2xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance with 8 dedicated cores, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a 4 Gbps baseline network speed.
tagline: An ARM-based general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated physical cores, high-bandwidth memory, and balanced compute performance for diverse enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 8 dedicated AWS Neoverse-V2 ARM cores running at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory and 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier memory performance and strong single-threaded compression capabilities
Tradeoffs include lower performance in cryptographic operations and large LLM inference
Best suited for web serving, database operations, caching, and software compilation
